exemplar.title: How does pH (3, 5, 7, 9, 11) affect the equilibrium adsorption capacity of 10 ppm methylene blue onto 0.5g of granular activated carbon, and which kinetic model, pseudo-first-order or pseudo-second-order, best describes this process?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does pH (3, 5, 7, 9, 11) affect the equilibrium adsorption capacity of 10 ppm methylene blue onto 0.5g of granular activated carbon, and which kinetic model, pseudo-first-order or pseudo-second-order, best describes this process?

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately with clear industrial and environmental rationale throughout the report
Research question is precisely formulated, quantitatively specifying all variables and guiding the investigation
Methodology is complete and replicable, with informed selection of trials, controls, and justification of choices
Accurate and consistent use of technical terminology (e.g., âequilibrium adsorption capacityâ, âpseudo-first-orderâ) ensures clear focus
weaknesses: Occasional interchange of âadsorbent/absorbentâ introduces minor terminological inaccuracy
Background section is somewhat lengthy, diluting focus at times
Apparatus precision (e.g., spectrophotometer range, error margins) is not fully described
Duplicate restatements of the research question add unnecessary length
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently communicates the topic with clear purpose and maintains focus directly on the research question. The methodology section is thorough and demonstrates effective selection of methods and controls. Minor lapses in term consistency and background length do not detract from the overall clarity and coherence, placing this criterion firmly in the top band.

strengths: Demonstrates clear, coherent subject knowledge of adsorption chemistry, kinetic modelling, and pH effects
Terminology and concepts are used accurately (e.g., chemisorption, electrostatic repulsion)
Equations (qâ, RÂ² calculations) are presented in standard form
Integration of diverse sources to discuss activated carbon structure shows good research depth
weaknesses: Inconsistent use of terminology (e.g., âabsorbentâ vs. âadsorbentâ) detracts from precision
Some citations are too generic (e.g., âBritannicaâ without authorâdate)
Application of sources is uneven, with occasional paraphrased compilations rather than critical integration
Limited treatment of error, uncertainty, and formal statistical discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows solid understanding and coherent use of subjectâspecific terminology and concepts. Source material is generally relevant, though integration could be more critical and precise. Minor lapses in citation style and error discussion prevent a higher mark but performance clearly aligns with the upper half of the 3â4 band.

strengths: Primary data are substantial and consistently relevant to the research question (multiâpH trials, kinetics)
Literature is used effectively to justify design choices and contextualize results
Analysis is clear, with correct calculations, RÂ² interpretation, and chemical reasoning
Conclusion answers both parts of the question and links findings to theory
Evaluation acknowledges key methodological limitations and offers concrete improvements
weaknesses: Error analysis is largely qualitative, with limited statistical treatment or uncertainty propagation
Reliance on tertiary sources restricts depth; primary peer-reviewed articles could strengthen discussion
Comparisons of calculated qâ values with literature benchmarks are minimal
Alternative interpretations of data are only briefly explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits good to very good research quality, clear and well-supported analysis, and a solid critical evaluation of methods and results. While a more rigorous statistical treatment and deeper engagement with primary literature would elevate it further, overall performance sits at the top of the 7â9 band.

strengths: Structure follows all Extended Essay conventions (title page, contents, sections, bibliography, appendix)
Layout is readerâfriendly with consistent formatting, numbered pages, and properly labelled figures/tables
Citation style is applied correctly throughout
weaknesses: Figure captions sometimes lack sufficient explanatory detail (e.g., structural relevance)
Equations are unnumbered and not always referenced in the text
Inline math formatting interrupts the flow in variable definitions
The variables table is visually dense and could be simplified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organization and layout fully meet expectations for structure and presentation. Minor formatting and caption details do not impede readability, placing this work at the top of the presentation band.

strandSelections.reasoning: The structure follows EE conventions: title page with word count, abstract omitted per new guide, contents page, sequential sections, bibliography and appendix.  Headings and sub-headings are logical and facilitate navigation.  All major structural elements are present and appropriate, justifying the top of the 3â4 band.

The topic of pH-dependent adsorption of methylene blue on activated carbon is introduced with a clear industrial and environmental rationale, and the student maintains this focus throughout the introduction, methodology, results, and conclusion.  Terminology such as âequilibrium adsorption capacityâ, âpseudo-first-orderâ, and âpseudo-second-orderâ is used correctly to frame the purpose, showing an accurate and purposeful explanation.  Minor lapsesâe.g., occasional interchange of âadsorbent/absorbentâ and a somewhat lengthy background that dilutes focusâprevent a full-marks judgement, but the overall communication is consistently clear and effective.

Calculations of concentrations, percentage removal and kinetic parameters are carried out correctly; graphs include RÂ² values, and trends are interpreted with chemical reasoning.  However, error analysis is qualitative, statistical treatment limited, and some calculated qâ values are not compared with literature.  The analysis is therefore clear and mostly well-supported but not consistently rigorous, aligning with the top of the 4â6 band.

The investigation is grounded in substantial primary data (five repeats across five pH levels plus kinetic trials) that are directly relevant to the stated question.  Literature is used to justify design choices and contextualise results, showing goodâthough not exhaustiveâresearch coverage.  Some important variables (carbon characterisation, temperature) are acknowledged but not explored, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellentâ 10â12 level.

The essay demonstrates clear and coherent understanding of adsorption chemistry, kinetic modelling, and pH effects.  Calculations of qâ, RÂ² interpretation, and linkages to functional-group chemistry indicate sound subject knowledge.  Minor misconceptions (e.g. calling AC an âabsorbentâ; limited treatment of error/uncertainty) show that understanding, while good, is not fully authoritative.  Thus the upper mark of the 3â4 band is appropriate.

Key chemistry termsââchemisorptionâ, âelectrostatic repulsionâ, âequilibrium adsorption capacityââare mostly employed correctly, and equations are presented using standard notation.  Nonetheless, some inconsistent wording (âabsorbent/adsorbentâ, "qÏ" typos) and formatting slips reduce precision and consistency, so performance sits at the mid-band level.

The student presents a complete, replicable methodology: calibration curve, two-stage experimental design, detailed control of variables, risk assessment, and justification of choices (granular vs powdered AC, 4-min intervals).  Multiple trials (n=5 for equilibrium, n=3 for kinetics) demonstrate informed selection.  Weaknesses noted in the evaluation (unknown pore size; single initial dye concentration) show some methodological limitations, so full marks are not warranted, but the range and clarity support a strong band 5.

The layout is reader-friendly: consistent font, labelled tables/figures, numbered pages referenced in the contents, and correct citation style.  Minor issues (occasional line-break artefacts, small figure captions) are present but do not impede understanding.  Consequently, the work meets all layout expectations at a high standard.

The conclusion answers both parts of the research question and links findings to functional-group behaviour and kinetic theory.  The evaluation recognises methodological limitations (unknown pore size, low absorbance readings) and suggests concrete improvements.  Nonetheless, critique of data reliability (e.g., uncertainty propagation, repeatability statistics) is limited, and alternative interpretations are only briefly touched upon.  This gives a solid but not fully critical performance within the 4â6 band.

Source material is generally relevant (textile-dye pollution, surface chemistry of AC, kinetic theory) and cited in-text; the extensive bibliography reflects breadth.  However, integration is unevenâseveral citations are tutorial videos or general websites, and background passages occasionally read as paraphrased compilations rather than critically applied evidence.  Application in the discussion is adequate but not consistently strong, placing performance solidly in the 3â4 band at the lower end.

The research question is explicitly stated twice, quantitatively specifying pH values, dye concentration, carbon mass, and the kinetic comparison.  This tight formulation guides the whole report, is revisited in the evaluation, and is neither too broad nor too narrow for an EE.  Some subsidiary aims (e.g., percentage removal) are included without being formally framed as sub-questions, so the focus is strong but not flawless, meriting the lower end of the top band.

annotations.comment: The initial planning reflection clearly shows motivation but could include a more explicit hypothesis development to strengthen the research focus.
The title is concise and informative, effectively communicating the investigationâs scope and variables.
The research question is precisely formulated with all variables and models clearly specified, guiding the study with strong focus.
Word count is stated and falls within the limit, satisfying the formatting requirement.
Citations like â(Britannica)â are too generic; adopt a consistent authorâdate style and ensure full reference details are in-text.
The reflection outlines useful skills but remains descriptive; consider adding specific examples of how these skills will shape your future experimental planning and decision-making.
The second meeting reflection shows progress but lacks analysis of challenges overcome; add evaluative commentary on how your supervisorâs guidance influenced method refinement.
Include characterization data of your activated carbon (e.g., surface area, pore size) to strengthen the background context.
Reliance on tertiary sources (e.g., STS Canada) limits depth; incorporate primary peer-reviewed articles for detailed adsorbent chemistry.
Contextualizing MB as a major pollutant strengthens relevance to environmental chemistry.
The summary of MBâs toxicity is informative but lacks critical assessment; discuss how these findings directly motivate your experimental design.
The description of adsorption mechanisms is clear, but link this more explicitly to how pH modulates these interactions in your own study.
Terminology is used correctly (adsorbent, adsorbate), demonstrating solid conceptual understanding.
Inconsistent use of âabsorbentâ instead of âadsorbentâ appears; ensure precise terminology throughout.
The background on kinetic models is comprehensive but lengthy; condense this section to maintain focus on the research question.
Integration of diverse sources to discuss AC structure shows good research depth.
Figure 1 caption lacks detailed explanation of structural relevance; expand to explain how resonance affects adsorption behavior.
The PFO kinetic equation is clearly presented; consider adding units for q_t (mgÂ·gâ»Â¹) and t (min) in the equation caption.
Citing literature for equilibrium contact time selection demonstrates informed methodology design.
Check the chemical formula formatting CââHââClNâS; ensure correct element order and subscripts.
The detailed discussion of ÏâÏ and electrostatic interactions is well articulated, linking molecular forces to adsorption.
Equations should be numbered and referenced in the text to improve clarity and reader navigation.
The variable definitions section is interrupted by inline math formatting; reformat to ensure smoother readability.
The research question is restated clearly, but duplicate statements add length; retain the first occurrence for conciseness.
The variables table is comprehensive but visually dense; merge related headings and add shading or lines to distinguish sections.
The preliminary procedure description omits spectrophotometer wavelength range limitations; include apparatus precision and error considerations.
Justify the choice of three consecutive unchanged absorbance readings for defining equilibrium with supporting rationale.
